# Hash Generator

> Free online cryptographic hash generator for MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512. Generate hashes from text or files instantly with complete privacy and security.

## Overview

Hash Generator is a privacy-first, browser-based tool for creating cryptographic hashes. All hashing operations occur entirely in your browser—no data is sent to external servers, ensuring complete privacy and security.

**What it does:**
- Generate MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512 hashes from text input
- Hash files up to 100MB without uploading to servers
- Verify file integrity by comparing hashes
- Save hash generation history in browser storage
- Download hashes with one-click copying

**Key features:**
- No server uploads—100% browser-based processing
- Support for five industry-standard hash algorithms
- Dark mode and light mode themes
- Full keyboard navigation and WCAG AA accessibility
- Responsive design for desktop, tablet, and mobile
- Free, open-source, no registration required

## Why Use Hash Generator?

Hash generators are essential for:
- **File integrity verification** — Confirm files haven't been corrupted or tampered with
- **Password security** — Understand how modern web apps securely store passwords
- **Software distribution** — Verify downloaded software matches publisher hashes
- **Digital signatures** — Authenticate documents and transactions
- **Blockchain** — Explore SHA-256 hashing used in Bitcoin and Ethereum
- **Data verification** — Ensure checksums match across systems and transfers

## Target Audience

- **Developers** — Verify file checksums and learn cryptographic hashing
- **Security professionals** — Test and demonstrate hash algorithms
- **System administrators** — Generate file integrity checksums
- **Students** — Learn how cryptographic hashing works
- **Privacy-conscious users** — Hash data without cloud uploads
- **Anyone verifying downloads** — Ensure file authenticity

## Key Topics

- Cryptographic hashing
- MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512 algorithms
- File integrity verification
- Hash verification and comparison
- Browser-based data processing
- Data privacy and security
- Algorithm selection and best practices
- File checksums and downloads
- Password hashing and security
- Blockchain and cryptocurrency hashing

## FAQ

**Is my data safe?**
Yes. All hashing happens in your browser. No data is sent to servers.

**Which algorithm should I use?**
SHA-256 for most purposes. SHA-512 for maximum security. Avoid MD5 and SHA-1 for cryptographic applications.

**How large can files be?**
Up to 100MB, though 50MB or less is recommended for best performance.

**Can I verify hashes match?**
Yes, use the "Verify Hash" tab to compare two hashes.

**Is it free?**
Completely free and open-source.

## Technical Details

- **Platform:** Web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, etc.)
- **Technology:** Pure JavaScript (no backend servers)
- **Algorithms:** TweetNaCl.js / built-in Web Crypto API
- **License:** MIT
- **Source:** GitHub (open-source)

## Get Started

1. Paste text or upload a file
2. Select hash algorithms (SHA-256 recommended)
3. Generate hashes instantly
4. Copy, compare, or verify results

No installation, registration, or internet uploads required.
